input.prompt:
Please help me analyze `/Users/caspian/helmor-dev/workspaces/helmor/jupiter/` thisproject's root file, findwhich files may no longer be useful (deprecated, redundant, outdated etc).

Please do the following:
1. List all files in root file (not including subdirectory contents, only root-level files)
2. For each file, check:
   - whether it is referenced/imported by other files
   - whether it matches the project's current tech stack (project is Tauri v2 + React 19 + Vite + TypeScript）
   - whether there are duplicate functionality files
   - whether config files are still effective (e.g. whether there are deprecated build tool configs)
   - lock  files, temporary files etc
3. Focus on checking: root file config files, script files, documentation fi les etc

Only do analysis, do not make any modifications. Please give a detailed analysis report, including each suspicious fi le's reason.

content.content:
{
	"name": "helmor",
	"private": true,
	"version": "0.1.0",
	"packageManager": "pnpm@10.13.1",
	"type": "module",
	"scripts": {
		"dev": "tauri dev",
		"dev:vite": "vite",
		"dev:api": "cd src-tauri && cargo run --bin helmor-dev-server --features dev-server",
		"analyze:web": "VITE_HELMOR_ANALYZE=1 vite",
		"analyze": "VITE_HELMOR_ANALYZE=1 HELMOR_LOG=debug tauri dev",
		"build": "tsc && vite build",
		"preview": "vite preview",
		"test": "vitest run",
		"test:watch": "vitest",
		"tauri": "tauri",
		"tauri:debug": "HELMOR_LOG=debug tauri dev",
		"lint": "biome check . --config-path=./biome.json && cargo clippy --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- -D warnings",
		"lint:fix": "biome check . --write --config-path=./biome.json && cargo clippy --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --fix --allow-dirty -- -D warnings && cargo fmt --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --all",
		"prepare": "husky"
	},
	"dependencies": {
		"@base-ui/react": "^1.3.0",
		"@fontsource-variable/geist": "^5.2.8",
		"@primer/octicons-react": "^19.23.1",
		"@radix-ui/react-avatar": "^1.1.11",
		"@radix-ui/react-scroll-area": "^1.2.10",
		"@radix-ui/react-tabs": "^1.1.13",
		"@radix-ui/react-toast": "^1.2.15",
		"@radix-ui/react-tooltip": "1.2.8",
		"@tanstack/react-query": "^5.96.2",
		"@tauri-apps/api": "2.10.1",
		"@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog": "2.6.0",
		"@tauri-apps/plugin-opener": "2.5.3",
		"@testing-library/user-event": "14.6.1",
		"class-variance-authority": "^0.7.1",
		"clsx": "^2.1.1",
		"lucide-react": "^1.7.0",
		"monaco-editor": "^0.55.1",
		"react": "19.2.0",
		"react-dom": "19.2.0",
		"react-loading-skeleton": "^3.5.0",
		"react-virtuoso": "^4.18.4",
		"shadcn": "^4.1.2",
		"shiki": "^4.0.2",
		"streamdown": "^2.5.0",
		"tailwind-merge": "^3.5.0",
		"tw-animate-css": "^1.4.0"
	},
	"devDependencies": {
		"@biomejs/biome": "^2.4.10",
		"@tailwindcss/vite": "4.2.2",
		"@tauri-apps/cli": "2.10.1",
		"@testing-library/jest-dom": "6.9.1",
		"@testing-library/react": "16.3.2",
		"@types/react": "19.2.14",
		"@types/react-dom": "19.2.3",
		"@vitejs/plugin-react": "6.0.1",
		"husky": "^9.1.7",
		"jsdom": "29.0.1",
		"lint-staged": "^16.4.0",
		"tailwindcss": "4.2.2",
		"typescript": "6.0.2",
		"vite": "8.0.3",
		"vitest": "4.1.2"
	},
	"lint-staged": {
		"*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,json,css}": [
			"biome check --write --error-on-warnings --no-errors-on-unmatched --files-ignore-unknown=true --config-path=./biome.json"
		],
		"*.rs": [
			"bash -c 'cargo fmt --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml --all'",
			"bash -c 'cargo clippy --manifest-path src-tauri/Cargo.toml -- -D warnings'"
		]
	}
}
[rerun: b2]

content.content:
# Helmor

Brush version 1 of a local-first desktop app built with Tauri, React, Vite, and
Vitest.

## Scripts

- `pnpm install` installs dependencies.
- `pnpm run tauri dev` starts the desktop app in development mode.
- `pnpm run build` builds the frontend bundle.
- `pnpm run test` runs the current regression test suite.
- `pnpm run test:watch` starts Vitest in watch mode.

## Current Scope

This first brush intentionally keeps the app simple:

- one Tauri window
- one minimal React application shell
- one baseline test to lock the startup UI
[rerun: b17]

# Helmor

Brush version 1 of a local-first desktop app built with Tauri, React, Vite, and
Vitest.

## Scripts

- `pnpm install` installs dependencies.
- `pnpm run tauri dev` starts the desktop app in development mode.
- `pnpm run build` builds the frontend bundle.
- `pnpm run test` runs the current regression test suite.
- `pnpm run test:watch` starts Vitest in watch mode.

## Current Scope

This first brush intentionally keeps the app simple:

- one Tauri window
- one minimal React application shell
- one baseline test to lock the startup UI

delta.thinking: Now

I have a good picture of the project. Let me analyze which files in the root file might be unnecessary:

1. **
`public/vite.svg`** - Only referenced in `index.html` as the favicon (`
<link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/vite.svg" />`), and `public/tauri.svg` is not referenced
anywhere. These are the default Tauri + Vite template files
and should be replaced with the actual app icon. The `index.html` title is still "
Tauri + React + Typescript" which is also a template default.

. **`public/tauri.svg`** - Completely unused in the codebase.
3. **`index.html`** - The title and
favicon are template defaults that need updating, but the file itself is essential

.
4. **`.context/notes.md` and `.context/todos.md`** - Both are empty placeholder files with just headers

.
5. **`.env.example`** - Contains a GitHub client ID configuration template.
The
`.env.example` variable is actually used in `src-tauri/src/models/auth.rs`, so it serves
a purpose documenting that environment variable. The `README.md` has
some outdated references and commands that should be refreshed.
`CLAUDE.md` is a symlink to `AGENTS.md`, which is a normal setup for Claude Code
integration. The sidecar file has its own `bun.lock` since it's a separate project
using bun while the main project uses pnpm.
Looking
at what's actually unnecessary, I can identify a few categories
: the `public/tauri.svg` isn't referenced anywhere and should be removed,
while `.context/notes.md` and `.context/todos.md` are just empty placeholder files. The `public/vite.svg` is only there
as a default Vite favicon and should be replaced with an actual Helmor icon. The `index.html` still has the generic Vite template title that needs
updating to reflect the actual project.
The `README
.md` is outdated with references to "Brush version 1" and incorrect run commands. I
should note that `sidecar/bun.lock` is intentionally there since the
sidecar uses bun rather than pnpm, and `tsconfig.node.json` is standard Vite setup. The `.env.example` file

content.thinking:
Now I have a good picture of the project. Let me analyze which files in the root file might be unnecessary:

1. **`public/vite.svg`** - Only referenced in `index.html` as the favicon (`<link rel="icon" type="image/svg+xml" href="/vite.svg" />`), and `public/tauri.svg` is not referenced anywhere. These are the default Tauri + Vite template files and should be replaced with the actual app icon. The `index.html` title is still "Tauri + React + Typescript" which is also a template default.


2. **`public/tauri.svg`** - Completely unused in the codebase.
3. **`index.html`** - The title and favicon are template defaults that need updating, but the file itself is essential.
4. **`.context/notes.md` and `.context/todos.md`** - Both are empty placeholder files with just headers.
5. **`.env.example`** - Contains a GitHub client ID configuration template.

The `.env.example` variable is actually used in `src-tauri/src/models/auth.rs`, so it serves a purpose documenting that environment variable. The `README.md` has some outdated references and commands that should be refreshed. `CLAUDE.md` is a symlink to `AGENTS.md`, which is a normal setup for Claude Code integration. The sidecar file has its own `bun.lock` since it's a separate project using bun while the main project uses pnpm.

Looking at what's actually unnecessary, I can identify a few categories: the `public/tauri.svg` isn't referenced anywhere and should be removed, while `.context/notes.md` and `.context/todos.md` are just empty placeholder files. The `public/vite.svg` is only there as a default Vite favicon and should be replaced with an actual Helmor icon. The `index.html` still has the generic Vite template title that needs updating to reflect the actual project.

The `README.md` is outdated with references to "Brush version 1" and incorrect run commands. I should note that `sidecar/bun.lock` is intentionally there since the sidecar uses bun rather than pnpm, and `tsconfig.node.json` is standard Vite setup. The `.env.example` file might be unnecessary depending on whether GitHub auth is still an active feature.
